feedback.text: For whom it may concern. I have played video games for almost 30 years. While the cartridges and discs on my shelves are mostly safe, digital games and ones requiring a online connection to work, or stuck on locked-down platforms are not as protected. While my personal games are safe for now, this is not guaranteed to continue. I hope a solution can be found that safe-guards my ability to continue to enjoy the games I purchased and enjoyed long into the future. This includes issues such as games being unable to be played in advertised single-player modes due to missing publisher server and unable to make backups of purchased software due to locked-down platforms and no ability to redownload due to closed store-fronts. Potential solutions I can see, include requirements to provide true offline-mode for games where such is resonable, either at launch or as later patch before support is removed. For online store-fronts, I would like to see offline options to create backups of software that is not locked to specific devices. If my games console dies due to failing components, I feel that I should be able to restore my digital collection on a equivalent machine. If a game is not worth selling, is it worth enforcing digital protections against piracy if it hinders me from enjoying the game on my own hardware, just because it was not available to be registered to some online account when the service was still active? Thank you for listening to my concerns on this topic. I hope to hear good news in this area soon.
